title: Orbital Window
description: Orbital Window is an aerospace observation dashboard that helps people search a city, compare recognizable satellites, and decide when a pass is actually worth stepping outside for using orbital data, cloud cover, and saved observing sites.
mainImageAlt: Orbital Window dashboard showing a satellite selection strip, orbit map, and upcoming pass cards for Casablanca.

children.text: Orbital Window turns satellite tracking into a practical, layman-friendly interface. Instead of making people decipher raw orbital data, it answers the question they actually care about: is there something worth watching from here tonight?

The app combines live TLE data from CelesTrak, location search through Nominatim, and cloud cover plus sunrise and sunset data from Open-Meteo. It then scores upcoming passes so the experience feels like a decision-making tool rather than a raw telemetry viewer.

From a portfolio perspective, the project demonstrates aerospace curiosity, external API integration, local persistence with SQLite/libsql and Drizzle, custom data processing with satellite.js, and a polished frontend that is understandable to non-technical users.
